title: Received a Section 245 notice? Here's why your income tax refund may be adjusted against pending tax demand
summary: A Section 245 intimation is issued when the Income Tax Department proposes to adjust your refund against an outstanding tax demand from the previous year. Taxpayers can check the demand details on the e-filing portal and respond by accepting the demand or disputing it within the given timeline.

title: New Income Tax Act: CBDT issues FAQs on pending tax cases, TDS and transition rules
summary: CBDT has clarified how pending tax cases, assessments, and TDS certificate applications will move after the Income-tax Act 2025 takes effect from April 2026. The FAQs explain which matters stay under the old law and which follow new provisions.

title: FCNR deposits: a compelling opportunity for NRIs
summary: That combination of high foreign-exchange returns, no exchange-rate risk, and tax-free interest in India makes FCNR compelling.

title: EPS Scheme 2026 vs EPS 1995: What changes for employees under new pension framework?
summary: The EPS 2026 has replaced EPS 1995, introducing a new pension framework under the Code on Social Security, 2020. The new scheme retains key pension benefits and contribution rates while adding digital compliance, a 20-day claim settlement timeline and interest on delayed claims.&nbsp;

title: Why Gen Z borrows for experiences and keeps investments intact
summary: With higher financial literacy, younger borrowers are leveraging personal loans to fund lifestyle choices, experiences, travel and discretionary spending, all while keeping their investment portfolios intact.

title: What Parag Parikh Flexi Cap Fund bought and sold in June 2026: Top 10 holdings revealed
summary: Parag Parikh Flexi Cap Fund is India's largest open-ended equity scheme with an AUM of ₹1.43 lakh crore. The fund increased its exposure to HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank and ITC in June, while trimming its holdings in Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon and Meta Platforms.&nbsp;

title: 8th Pay Commission Kolkata meeting begins today: What Central government employees and pensioners can expect
summary: The 8th Pay Commission begins stakeholder consultations in Kolkata today, with discussions likely to focus on fitment factor, minimum pay, pension reforms and HRA revisions. The feedback will shape final recommendations for central government staff and pensioners.

title: July 2026 DA hike: 3% increase most likely as June inflation data guides decision, says expert
summary: 3% Dearness Allowance hike for Central government staff and pensioners is a possibility in July 2026. The final DA revision hinges on June AICPI-IW inflation data, with the government's announcement expected after Cabinet approval and inflation review.

title: UPI and personal loans are replacing credit cards in Indians' wallets: CIBIL
